entries.label: Basic closure
entries.code:
let add = |a, b| a + b;
let result = add(2, 3); // 5
entries.note: Parameters in |...|; types usually inferred

entries.label: With type annotations
entries.code: let add = |a: i32, b: i32| -> i32 { a + b };
entries.note: Explicit types optional but helpful for clarity

entries.label: No parameters
entries.code:
let greet = || println!("hello");
greet();
entries.note: Empty || for closures with no parameters

entries.label: Multi-line body
entries.note: Use braces for multiple statements; last expression is return value

entries.label: Single expression
entries.code:
let square = |x: i32| x * x;
let is_even = |x: &i32| x % 2 == 0;
entries.note: Braces optional for single expression closures

entries.label: Closure captures variables
entries.note: Closures capture variables from their enclosing scope by reference

entries.label: Capture by reference (&T)
entries.note: Default for immutable access; borrows from environment

entries.label: Capture by mutable reference (&mut T)
entries.note: Default when closure mutates the captured variable

entries.label: Capture by value (move)
entries.note: move keyword forces ownership transfer; required for thread::spawn

entries.label: Move with Copy types
entries.note: move copies Copy types; moves non-Copy types

entries.label: Selective capture
entries.note: Closures capture the smallest amount needed; capture specific references

entries.label: Move closure for threads
entries.note: move required for spawn because closure must be 'static (own all data)

entries.label: Fn (immutable borrow)
entries.note: Fn: can be called multiple times; captures by &T; most restrictive

entries.label: FnMut (mutable borrow)
entries.note: FnMut: can be called multiple times; captures by &mut T

entries.label: FnOnce (take ownership)
entries.note: FnOnce: can only be called once; captures by value; least restrictive bound

entries.label: Trait hierarchy
entries.note: Fn: FnMut: FnOnce -- every Fn is FnMut, every FnMut is FnOnce

entries.label: Choosing the right bound
entries.note: Accept the least restrictive bound your function needs

entries.label: impl Fn syntax
entries.note: Most common way; monomorphized at compile time

entries.label: Generic bound
entries.note: Equivalent to impl Fn; needed when same bound used multiple times

entries.label: Where clause
entries.note: Cleaner for complex bounds

entries.label: dyn Fn (trait object)
entries.note: Dynamic dispatch; use when you need to store different closures uniformly

entries.label: Function pointer
entries.note: fn() type for plain functions (not closures that capture environment)

entries.label: Closure vs function pointer
entries.note: fn pointers are for closures without captures; Fn traits for all closures

entries.label: Return impl Fn
entries.note: Return opaque closure type; single concrete type only

entries.label: Return Box<dyn Fn>
entries.note: Box<dyn Fn> when returning different closures based on logic

entries.label: Returning FnMut
entries.note: Return a stateful closure that mutates its captured state

entries.label: Closure in struct
entries.note: Store closure in a struct with generic parameter

entries.label: Boxed closure in struct
entries.note: Use Box<dyn Fn> when struct must hold different closure types

entries.label: Iterator adaptors
entries.note: Closures are the backbone of iterator chains

entries.label: Sort with closure
entries.note: Custom sorting logic via closures

entries.label: Lazy initialization
entries.note: Closure called once lazily; result cached

entries.label: Error handling with closures
entries.note: Closures power Result and Option combinator chains

entries.label: Thread::spawn with closure
entries.note: Closures with move for transferring data to threads

entries.label: Immediately invoked
entries.note: IIFE pattern: define and call a closure in one expression
